lines: Patient is a 65 year old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
He presented to the emergency department with a 2 day history of fever and cough.
Vital signs on admission were temperature 38.5 C, blood pressure 140/80 mmHg, and heart rate 100 bpm.
Physical examination revealed crackles in the lungs and a tender abdomen.
Laboratory results showed a white blood cell count of 15,000 cells/microliter and a lactate level of 1.5 mmol/L.
Chest X-ray showed a left lower lobe infiltrate.
The patient was started on antibiotics and oxygen therapy.
Urinalysis was negative for infection.
Blood cultures were sent and are pending.
The patient's creatinine level was 1.0 mg/dL on admission.
Platelet count was 150,000 cells/microliter.
The patient's MAP was 80 mmHg on admission.
The patient is being closely monitored for signs of sepsis.
The patient's temperature has been trending downward since admission.
The patient's oxygen saturation has been stable.
The patient's blood pressure has been stable.

The patient's condition is expected to continue to improve.
The patient's prognosis is good.
